details.text: Derivative: عَبُوسًا is the مبَالَغَة (emphasised) form of عَابِس which means to be gloomy, sad and sullen. عَبُوسًا means extremely gloomy, sad and sullen.
Derivative: زَمْهَرِيرًا refers to the severe and biting cold.
Derivative: قُطُوفُ refers to ripened fruit that is ready to be plucked.

Derivative: ذُلِّلَتْ from ذَلَّلَ – تَذْلِيلًا – which means to make something be humbled and to be tamed. Here, it means that the fruits will be made to serve them i.e., be lowered for them and be close at hand.
Derivative: آنِيَةٍ (Aaniyah) is the plural of إِناء (Inaa’) and refers to any type of container that food is placed inside of.
Derivative: قَوَارِيرَا refers to a container which is made from extremely fine and clear glass to contain drinks.

- In the case where قَوَارِيرَ (Qawaareer) usually refers to a container or goblet that is made from extremely fine and clear glass, here we are informed that instead of glass it will be made from silver which looks like glass in its fineness and clearness or transparency.

Derivative: سَلْسَبِيلًا refers to the name of a spring in paradise and it is derived from السُّلَاسَة (As-Sulaasah) which means to be easy and implies that the drink will be easy to swallow and will go down pleasantly and smoothly.
Derivative: أَمْشَاجٍ refers to a mixture that contains a variety of elements or qualities.
Derivative: شَدَدْنَا from شَدَّ which means to tighten, pull (together) and strengthen.
Derivative: أَسْرَ refers to that which binds the body together in terms of joints and ligaments etc.
- “We tightened their joints” means we made theirs builds and structures strong, firm and precise.

Derivative: يُفَجِّرُونَ from فَجَّرَ - تَفْجيرًا to explode and make burst forth or gush out. يُفَجِّرُونَه means: “They make it burst forth” i.e., it is under their command and will to make it burst forth when they wish it to.
Derivative: مُسْتَطِيرًا means be extremely widespread i.e., completely engulfing.
